name: Mykonos Restaurant Assassination
description: On 17 September 1992, gunmen entered the Mykonos Greek restaurant in Berlin and shot dead [Sadegh Sharafkandi](/people/sadegh-sharafkandi), Secretary-General of the [Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/democratic-party-of-iranian-kurdistan), along with PDKI representatives Fattah Abdoli and Homayoun Ardalan, and their translator Nouri Dehkordi. The assassination was carried out by Kazem Darabi, an Iranian national, and Abbas Rhayel, a Lebanese associate. The subsequent trial (1993–1997) produced a landmark verdict finding that the killings were ordered by the Committee for Special Operations, which included [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei), [Akbar Hashemi Rafsanjani](/people/akbar-hashemi-rafsanjani), [Ali Fallahian](/people/ali-fallahian), and [Ali Akbar Velayati](/people/ali-akbar-velayati). This was the first time a European court officially attributed state-sponsored terrorism to the highest levels of the Iranian government, triggering the recall of all EU ambassadors from Tehran.

sources.name: Human Rights Watch — State-sponsored assassinations report

editorial_notes: Landmark German court verdict 10 April 1997 implicated Khamenei, Rafsanjani, Fallahian, Velayati. All EU ambassadors recalled from Tehran.
